title: Welcome to Customer Support
full_text:
Emergency Exit Seats are available to Customers who:

Are able-bodied.
Are above 15 years of age on the date of travel.
Are not expectant mothers.
Are not classified as invalid (as defined in DGCA CAR - Section 8, Series O, Part VIII, Issue I)
Are not temporarily affected by an illness that impairs mobility.
Can:
Locate the emergency exit.
Recognize the emergency exit opening mechanism.
Understand the instructions for operating the emergency exit.
Operate the emergency exit.
Follow oral directions and hand signals given by a crew member.
Stow or secure the emergency exit door to allow use of the exit.
Swiftly move through the emergency exit
Assess, choose, and follow a safe path away/to the emergency exit.
Are not travelling with an infant.

FLY91 has the discretion to assign Emergency Exit Seats and other seats to Customers, considering prescribed security standards and the safety and comfort of all Customers on the flight.

If a Customer, who pre-booked an Emergency Exit Seat, does not meet the safety criteria for occupying such a seat, they may be requested to change their seat.

-->If you wish to change a seat in your existing booking, please click <<here>>.

-->To raise a specific query or concern, please submit your request using the following link and we will contact you: Submit Ticket

title: Welcome to Customer Support
full_text:
The process of purchasing an extra seat on a flight with Fly91 involves the following terms and conditions:
Buying an Extra Seat: An extra seat can only be purchased during the initial booking and cannot be added later to an existing booking. Bookings for an extra seat can only be done through FLY91 Customer Support.
Availability: The availability of an extra seat is subject to the airline's capacity.
Fare and Exclusions: The basic fare for the original seat and the extra seat is the same when purchased together. The extra seat fare excludes airport levies.
Baggage Allowance: Passengers buying an extra seat are entitled to 15 Kgs of free check-in baggage allowance. Additional charges may apply for excess baggage.
Codeshare and Interline Restrictions: An extra seat cannot be bought on a Fly91 Codeshare Flight or an Interline Booking.
Refund Policy: Refund of an extra seat is subject to the FLY91 cancellation policy.
No-Show Policy: In the event of a 'No-Show' or 'Gate No-Show,' the extra seat will be non-refundable.

By adhering to these conditions, passengers opting for an extra seat can enjoy the benefits of added personal space, comfort, and a more enjoyable flight experience.

-->To make a booking with an extra seat, please submit your request using the below link and we will contact you: Submit Ticket

title: Welcome to Customer Support
full_text:
Choose your favourite food and drinks before you fly.
Hot meals, snacks, and beverages available
Better choice when booked in advance
Limited availability onboard, so pre-booking is recommended

How to Add Extras?

While Booking Your Flight:
Simply select your extras while booking your ticket.

After Booking:
You can still add them later:
Go to Manage Booking
Enter your booking details
Choose your extras
Pay and confirm
A purchase receipt for on-board sale of snacks, beverages, and merchandise shall be issued to such Customer at the time of such sale.

-->To raise a specific query or concern, please submit your request using the following link and we will contact you: Submit Ticket

title: Welcome to Customer Support
full_text:
FLY91's policy regarding damaged, delayed, or lost Checked-in Baggage is as follows:

Liability Limitations:

FLY91's liability is subject to the limitations specified in the Carriage by Air Act, 1972, and any exceptions, adjustments, or amendments notified by the Ministry of Civil Aviation, Government of India.
For lost or damaged Checked-in Baggage, FLY91's liability is capped at INR 350/Kg, with a maximum liability of INR 20,000.
Liability for delayed Checked-in Baggage is determined at FLY91's discretion based on the prevailing policy.

Irregularity Report Requirement:

Customers must raise an irregularity report in case of baggage loss while at the destination airport upon arrival, not after departing.

Exemptions from Liability:

FLY91 will not provide compensation for indirect, consequential, or remote reasons related to lost, delayed, or damaged Checked-in Baggage.
FLY91 is not liable if the cause of loss, delay, or damage is the inherent defect or quality of the Checked-in Baggage or negligence on the part of the customers.
FLY91 is exempt from liability if it can demonstrate taking all reasonable measures to prevent loss, delay, or damage or if it was impossible to take such measures.
If customers' negligence or wrongful acts contributed to the loss, delay, or damage, FLY91 may be partially or fully exempt from liability.

Customer Responsibility:

Customers are entirely responsible for Hand Baggage and personal belongings, and FLY91 bears no liability for any loss or damage related to them.
FLY91 is not responsible for the loss or damage of cash, valuable, fragile, or perishable items.
FLY91 is not liable for wear and tear to Checked-in Baggage, including specific instances listed in the policy.
Customers are solely accountable for any loss or damage caused by their Checked-in Baggage to any person or property, including FLY91's property, and FLY91 will not be held liable to any third party in connection to this.

These provisions outline the terms and limitations regarding FLY91's liability for Checked-in Baggage, emphasizing customer responsibility and the conditions under which FLY91 may be exempt from liability.

-->To raise a specific mishandled baggage concern , please submit the details using the following link and we will contact you: Submit Ticket

title: Welcome to Customer Support
full_text:
Carriage of certain restricted items is an offence and may result in criminal prosecution under Section 10 of the Aircraft Act 1934. Any baggage or person carrying dangerous goods is subject to be reported as per rule 9A of the Aircraft (Carriage of Dangerous Goods) Rules, 2003.

FLY91 reserves the right to refuse carriage of Baggage with certain restricted items. A description of such items is set out below:

Items inadequately packaged, lacking proper suitcases or suitable containers.

Items likely to jeopardise the aircraft, individuals, or property on board, as stipulated in the International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) Technical Instructions for the Safe Transport of Dangerous Goods by Air, IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations, and dangerous goods in accordance with local laws, applicable regulations, and Fly91's terms and conditions.

Items whose carriage is prohibited by prevailing laws, regulations, or orders.

Fly91 enforces a strict prohibition on the carriage of firearms, air guns, and ammunition on its flights. This includes various portable weapons like pistols, revolvers, rifles, shotguns, and similar devices capable of projecting projectiles. For passengers with legitimate needs to carry firearms or ammunition, we suggest considering alternative carriage methods that adhere to the safety and regulatory standards established by the appropriate authorities.
Gases like compressed gases, liquefied gases, refrigerated liquefied gases, dissolved gases, flammable, non-flammable, and poisonous gases, including butane, oxygen, liquid nitrogen, avalanche rescue backpacks, and medical oxygen.

Flammable liquids and solids such as lighter refills, lighter fuel, flammable paints, thinners, used camping stoves or fuel containers, used internal combustion or fuel cell engines, matches, fire-lighters, lighters requiring inversion before ignition or powered by lithium batteries, copra, sodium, potassium, etc.

Oxidising substances and organic peroxides, e.g., bleaches, aluminium nitrate, etc.

Toxic and infectious substances like pesticides, insecticides, weed-killers, potassium cyanide, samples for infectious disease testing, etc.

Radioactive material.

Corrosives such as acids, alkalis, mercury, wet cell batteries, and apparatus containing mercury.

Other dangerous substances and articles, including environmentally hazardous substances like magnetised materials, materials with narcotic, noxious, offensive, or irritating properties, and e-cigarettes.

Small lithium battery-powered vehicles like air-wheels, solo-wheels, hoverboards, mini-segways, and balance wheels.

Anything (including food or consumables containing seafood or any animal or bird elements) improperly packed that could cause leakage, spillage, or discomfort to other Customers.

Anything emitting a conspicuous or offensive odour.

Live or dead animals, birds, or insects unless expressly permitted in the Conditions of Carriage.

Satellite phones and GPS devices unless proper authorisations have been obtained from BSNL for BSNL-provided INMARSAT satellite handsets (ISAT Phones), and from the Ministry of Home Affairs or Department of Telecommunications for other Satellite Phones or GPS devices, as applicable.

Items deemed unsuitable for carriage based on weight, shape, size, or nature, according to FLY91's reasonable judgement.

Any other item that may inconvenience or pose a safety hazard to Customers, at FLY91's sole discretion.

Any other item prohibited for carriage on board, as notified by the Bureau of Civil Aviation Security (BCAS) or any relevant authority.

-->To raise a specific query or concern, please submit your request using the following link and we will contact you: Submit Ticket

 

For further information, please click here - Conditions of Carriage

title: Welcome to Customer Support
full_text:
We understand that travel plans can change from time to time!

Change Flight:

1. You can change your flight at any time from the time you make the booking till 2 hours prior to the scheduled time of departure 

2. Change fees per passenger per sector will be applicable as outlined in the Tariff Sheet available on the FLY91 website. 

3. In case of change of date/flight, the fare difference (if any) will be applicable in addition to the Change fee.

Seat Change 

1. You can choose to change your seat at any time from the time you make the booking till 1 hour prior to the scheduled time of departure 

2. Please note that a Seat Fee may apply to certain seats across the aircraft. Passengers may opt to either purchase the seats available as per their choice, or opt for any available free seat, or alternatively, opt for random free seat assignment by the system.  

3. At any point in time, Fly91 cannot guarantee adjacent seats, or seats in the same row or in any preferential sequence for 2 or more passengers travelling together unless such seats are blocked or purchased basis availability at the time of making the booking. 

Cancel Booking 

1. You can perform any of the below cancellations in the booking from the time you make the booking till 2 hours prior to the scheduled time of departure: 

Cancel a single sector or flight 

Cancel multiple sectors or flights 

Cancel a single or more passengers within the booking 

Cancel the entire booking 

2. Cancellation Fees per passenger per sector will be applicable as outlined in the Tariff Sheet available on the FLY91 website. 

3. Convenience Fee is non-refundable 

4.  If a customer opts for a refund, it will be processed based on the payment method used for the booking and within specified timeframes.

No-Show

1. No changes can be made to bookings where the passenger is a no-show or fails to take the flight.

2. For no-show or missed flights, only the airport taxes will be refundable.

-->To modify or cancel your existing booking, please click <<here>>.

-->To raise a specific query or concern, please submit your request using the following link and we will contact you: Submit Ticket

For further information, please click here - Conditions of Carriage

sections.heading: Seat Change
sections.text: 1. You can choose to change your seat at any time from the time you make the booking till 1 hour prior to the scheduled time of departure 2. Please note that a Seat Fee may apply to certain seats across the aircraft. Passengers may opt to either purchase the seats available as per their choice, or opt for any available free seat, or alternatively, opt for random free seat assignment by the system. 3. At any point in time, Fly91 cannot guarantee adjacent seats, or seats in the same row or in any preferential sequence for 2 or more passengers travelling together unless such seats are blocked or purchased basis availability at the time of making the booking.

sections.heading: Cancel Booking
sections.text: 1. You can perform any of the below cancellations in the booking from the time you make the booking till 2 hours prior to the scheduled time of departure: Cancel a single sector or flight Cancel multiple sectors or flights Cancel a single or more passengers within the booking Cancel the entire booking 2. Cancellation Fees per passenger per sector will be applicable as outlined in the Tariff Sheet available on the FLY91 website. 3. Convenience Fee is non-refundable 4.  If a customer opts for a refund, it will be processed based on the payment method used for the booking and within specified timeframes.

sections.heading: No-Show
sections.text: 1. No changes can be made to bookings where the passenger is a no-show or fails to take the flight. 2. For no-show or missed flights, only the airport taxes will be refundable. -->To modify or cancel your existing booking, please click <<here>>. -->To raise a specific query or concern, please submit your request using the following link and we will contact you: Submit Ticket For further information, please click here - Conditions of Carriage

title: Welcome to Customer Support
full_text:
Fly91 does not permit alterations in the name once a Booking is finalised. However, minor corrections in the spelling of a Customer’s name, subject to submission of a valid Identification document, may be made within 24 (twenty-four) hours of making the Booking without incurring additional charges, by contacting Fly91.

-->To cancel and rebook an existing booking, please click <<here>>.

Minor spelling corrections can be done against the same booking within the 24 hour window subject to submission of a valid Identification document.

-->To raise a minor spelling correction request, please submit the details using the following link and we will contact you: Submit Ticket

title: Welcome to Customer Support
full_text:
FLY91 accepts various forms of payment for bookings, including credit cards, debit cards, net banking, UPI, and wallets.

The use of these payment methods is subject to the terms and conditions set by the respective payment platforms.
It is mandatory for Customers whose Bookings have been paid for using cards to carry the original card or a copy of the card used for making the Booking signed by the holder of such card, at the time of check-in.
It is important to note that FLY91 does not accept cheques as a mode of payment for bookings. 
Customers are encouraged to refer to the terms and conditions provided by the specific payment platforms for guidance on payment procedures.

For bookings made at FLY91's airports where cash is used as the mode of payment, the following conditions apply:

Overall Limit: Cash payments are subject to an overall limit of Rupees INR 1,99,999 (One Lakh Ninety-Nine Thousand Nine Hundred and Ninety-Nine) against any 1 (one) PNR (Passenger Name Record).

Excess Payment: Payments exceeding the overall limit are accepted but require the declaration of PAN (Permanent Account Number) details.

Regulatory Compliance: Transactions exceeding the overall limit, as per regulations, may need to be reported to relevant authorities for record-keeping purposes.

Customers making cash payments at the airport counters are advised to adhere to these limits and declarations to ensure compliance with FLY91's policies and regulatory requirements.

-->To raise a specific query or concern, please submit your request using the following link and we will contact you: Submit Ticket

title: Welcome to Customer Support
full_text:
FLY91 allows alterations or cancellations to bookings initiated at least 2 hours prior to the scheduled departure time subject to the applicable change / cancellation fees per passenger per sector detailed in the Tariff Sheet.

If a customer opts for a refund, it will be processed based on the payment method used for the booking and within specified timeframes. FLY91 may process refunds either to the individual who made the payment or the relevant travel agent, with proof required for refunds to a person not named in the booking.

Please note that bookings done through travel agencies are always refunded back to the concerned travel agency from where passengers make the booking. Kindly ensure that you contact your travel agency in case the airline has processed the refund, but the amount has not been refunded to you within 7 working days.

The refunds are made in INR, deducting any applicable fees and charges.

title: Welcome to Customer Support
full_text:
FLY91 has outlined the following conditions for the carriage of expectant mothers to safeguard their health and well-being:

Uncomplicated Single Pregnancy (Up to 36th week):

Expectant mothers in good health up to the 36th week of pregnancy may be accepted for carriage.
Those between 29 and 36 weeks are required to provide a medical certificate stating fitness to travel, the number of weeks of pregnancy, and expected date of delivery.
Expectant mothers beyond their 36th week will not be accepted for carriage.

Uncomplicated Multiple Pregnancy (Up to 32nd week):

Expectant mothers in good health up to the 32nd week of multiple pregnancies may be accepted for carriage.
Those between 29 and 32 weeks are required to provide a medical certificate from their consulting doctor.
Expectant mothers beyond their 32nd week will not be accepted for carriage.

Complicated Pregnancy (Single/Multiple) (Up to 32nd week):

Expectant mothers with complicated pregnancy (single or multiple) will be accepted until the 32nd week.
Those between 29 and 32 weeks with complicated pregnancy must get clearance from a FLY91 company doctor through the MEDA process.
Expectant mothers beyond their 32nd week will not be accepted for carriage.

All expectant mothers must sign an "Expectant Mother Declaration" during check-in, in addition to providing a fit-to-fly certificate from their treating/consulting doctor. The fit-to-fly certificate should confirm the absence of complications, fitness for the entire journey, no intended/voluntary stopover at transit points, and the absence of the expectation of premature delivery within the planned journey. Pregnant ladies beyond 36 weeks are not permitted to fly.

-->To raise a specific query or concern, please submit your request using the following link and we will contact you: Submit Ticket

title: Welcome to Customer Support
full_text:
Age Limit:

Infants under 7 days old are not allowed to travel.
An infant must be accompanied by an adult during travel.
Failure to provide valid proof of age may result in the child being considered as not an infant, and the full applicable tariff for the date of travel must be paid, subject to seat availability.

Seating:

No additional seats can be reserved for infants; they must be seated on an adult's lap.
A maximum of 5 infants are allowed on any FLY91 flight, and a customer traveling with an infant can book a seat, subject to availability.
Only one infant per adult customer is permitted for safety and operational requirements.
An infant fee, as specified in the Tariff Sheet is applicable per sector for traveling with an infant.

Baggage Allowance:

Customers traveling with infants are allowed an additional hand baggage allowance of up to 7 kgs.

These regulations are in place to ensure the safety and comfort of both infants and other passengers during air travel with Fly91.

-->To raise a specific query or concern, please submit your request using the following link and we will contact you: Submit Ticket

title: Welcome to Customer Support
full_text:
Small and harmless domestic pets, such as dogs and cats, accompanied by valid Health and Rabies vaccination certificates, may be accepted on FLY91 flights in the cabin at the owner’s risk, subject to the following conditions:

Booking Process:

Bookings for pets cannot be made through the website or travel agents.

Service Fee:

Carriage of pets (excluding Guide/Service Dogs) is subject to a service fee per Sector as outlined in the Tariff Sheet.

Container Requirements:

Pets (excluding Guide/Service Dogs) must be in a ventilated container with dimensions not exceeding 17”x10”x8”.
Total weight of the pet plus the container should not exceed 7 kgs.

Limitations:

A maximum of 01 pet is allowed per flight in the passenger cabin, subject to weight and container size restrictions.

Seating and Allocation:

Pets are not allowed to occupy a passenger seat.
Customers with pets will be allocated window seats.
Emergency Exit Row and premium seats are not allotted to customers with pets.

Storage and Restraints:

The container must be stowed under the seat at all times.
Trained guide dogs must be muzzled and restrained throughout the flight.
Pet dogs must be muzzled and restrained when taken out of the container.

Health and Vaccination:

Customers must carry the latest health and rabies vaccination certificates for their pets.
A moisture absorbent mat should be placed under the pet at all times.

Label and Indemnity:

A label indicating the type of pet must be affixed to the container.
Customers must complete the indemnity form provided by FLY91.

Notice and Reporting:

Customers must provide at least 24 hours' notice of their intent to carry pets.
Customers must report at least 2 hours before the scheduled departure time when traveling with a pet.

Restrictions and Considerations:

No food items are permitted in the container.
Certain breeds, like brachycephalic dogs, may have specific travel considerations.
Unaccompanied minors, customers requiring wheelchair assistance, and customers traveling with an infant cannot travel with pets.

Behaviour and Age Requirements:

A pet showing disruptive behaviour may be denied boarding.
Dogs and cats must be at least 3 months old, and pregnant pets of more than 4 weeks will not be carried.

Documentation and Declarations:

Documentation and declarations are required during drop-off and pick-up of a pet.

The customer assumes all risks associated with the pet's carriage and indemnifies FLY91 accordingly.

FLY91 reserves the right to refuse the carriage of a pet on its aircraft at its discretion. Customers are advised to carefully adhere to these guidelines and conditions when traveling with pets on FLY91 flights.

-->To add your pet to your booking, please submit your request using the following link and we will contact you: Submit Ticket

title: Welcome to Customer Support
full_text:
Children Aged 6-12 Years:

Must either be accompanied by a customer aged 18 or older or categorized as 'Unaccompanied Minors.'
FLY91 may allow Unaccompanied Minors to travel upon payment of applicable fees as published in the <Tariff Sheet>.

Departure:

Arrive at the airport at least 2 hours before the scheduled departure.
Parent or guardian submits a duly updated and signed copy of the <Unaccompanied Minor> form to FLY91 staff.
Valid photo identification required for both the minor and the parent or guardian at check-in.
A FLY91 staff member accompanies the minor to the aircraft, handing them over to cabin crew for supervision.
Parent or guardian must remain at the airport until the flight departs.

On-Board:

Cabin crew oversees the Unaccompanied Minor during the flight, ensuring safety compliance and access to amenities.
Parent/guardian is requested to pack necessary items in the Unaccompanied Minor's hand baggage.

Transit/Transfer:

  Acceptance of Unaccompanied Minors during stopovers/transfers depends on the duration and nature of the stopover.

  Stopovers exceeding 6 hours or involving an overnight stay or transfer to another airline may require adult accompaniment.

Upon Arrival:

  FLY91's ground staff accompanies and assists the Unaccompanied Minor through the airport arrival process.

  Handover to the nominated adult after verifying original photo identification.

  Parent or guardian should be at the arrival hall at least 30 minutes before the flight's arrival to receive the Unaccompanied Minor.

-->To book an Unaccompanied Minor, please submit your request using the following link and we will contact you: Submit Ticket

title: Welcome to Customer Support
full_text:
Customers requiring a wheelchair must request one at least 48 hours before the scheduled departure.

Complimentary Service:

FLY91's wheelchair service is provided at no additional charge to the Customer.
Assistance includes carrying the Customer within the airport area and to their designated seat on the aircraft.

Informing at Check-In:

Customers should inform the check-in counter if they need assistance before being escorted to the boarding gate.

Delays and Unpredictable Circumstances:

While FLY91 aims to assist Customers in reaching their onward flights on time, potential delays during busy periods are advised.
FLY91 is not liable for Customers who miss onward flights due to such delays but will accommodate needs to the best extent possible.
Unpredictable circumstances like adverse weather or mechanical issues may necessitate last-minute changes.

Group Wheelchair Requests:

Groups of 10 or more people requiring wheelchairs should notify FLY91 at least 48 hours before the scheduled departure.
FLY91 will allow the determined number of Customers to board based on seat availability and safety assistants (if required).

Assistance for Customers Without Safety Assistants:

FLY91 provides various assistance services for Customers traveling without a safety assistant.
Services include boarding, deplaning, flight connections, check-in/transfer/reclaiming of checked-in bags, stowing hand baggage, opening onboard snacks, and individual safety briefings.
FLY91 cannot assist with personal care, including feeding or administering medication, or accompany Customers to restrooms.

Use of Own Wheelchairs/Crutches/Prosthetic Devices:

Customers may use their own manual-powered wheelchair until the aircraft door or mobile stairway.
Electric wheelchairs will be dismantled at check-in counters, and the Customer will be transferred to FLY91's wheelchair.
Specific guidelines are provided for wheelchairs powered by wet-cell batteries, lithium-ion batteries, or non-spillable batteries.
Customers can use their own crutches, braces, and prosthetic devices up to the aircraft.
FLY91 makes efforts to return checked-in wheelchairs as close to the aircraft door as possible.
Alternative assistance is provided if necessary, such as electric buggies, airport wheelchairs, or accessible buses.
FLY91's liability for loss or damage to mobility aids is outlined, and customers are advised to obtain suitable travel insurance for their equipment.

Customers are encouraged to plan accordingly, considering potential delays and adhering to guidelines for the use of personal mobility aids.

-->If you wish to add a wheelchair service to your existing booking, please click <<here>>.

-->To notify us about your requirement, please submit your request using the following link and we will contact you: Submit Ticket

title: Welcome to Customer Support
full_text:
Please note that it is mandatory to possess a valid Restricted Area Permit (RAP) for all islands that passengers wish to visit and or have to visit in order to reach the intended destination. Please refer to the circular for more details - Circular

All foreign nationals may need additional permits. These permits can be arranged through authorised travel agents or the Lakshadweep Tourism website https://rb.gy/oj0gay

title: Welcome to Customer Support
full_text:
AirSewa is an initiative by the Ministry of Civil Aviation (MoCA), Government of India offering Digital grievance reporting and tracking system for airports, flights and other matters related to air travel.

Please visit the AirSewa website at https://airsewa.gov.in/home for more information.

title: Welcome to Customer Support
full_text:
Gate Closure Time:

1. Boarding gates will close 25 minutes before the scheduled departure time.

Customer Arrival:

1. Customers must arrive at the boarding gate no later than the time indicated during check-in or as announced at the airport.

2. Checking in or receiving a boarding pass does not guarantee boarding; compliance with all requirements, including specified timelines, is essential.

Announcements and Communication:

1. Announcements regarding changes in the boarding gate or schedule will be made through airport sound systems, digital screens, or notifications.

2. Customers understand that announcements may not be made at airports categorized as 'silent,' and it is their responsibility to check the flight information display system.

Responsibility of Customers:

1. Customers acknowledge that FLY91 or its staff is not obligated to contact them if they fail to be present at the boarding gate according to specified timelines.

2. FLY91 bears no liability for any Customer who fails to report to the boarding gate for any reason.

Gate No-Show Policy:

1. Customers failing to report to the boarding gate within the mentioned timelines will be considered a "Gate No-Show."

2. Customers are urged to adhere to the specified timelines for a timely departure. It is essential to stay informed about announcements and boarding gate details, especially in cases where airports are categorized as 'silent.' Failure to comply may result in being marked as a "Gate No-Show," and FLY91 holds no liability in such instances.

-->To raise a specific query or concern, please submit your request using the following link and we will contact you: Submit Ticket

title: Welcome to Customer Support
full_text:
At FLY91, you do not go through the hassles of performing a web check-in closer to departure! Once a booking is paid for and confirmed, passengers are auto checked-in for their flights and a Boarding Pass is issued immediately to the registered mobile number through WhatsApp as well as the registered email address once the booking is completed. 

-->To obtain a copy of your boarding pass for your upcoming flight, please click <<here>>.

-->To raise a specific query or concern, please submit your request using the following link and we will contact you: Submit Ticket

For further information, please click here - Conditions of Carriage

title: Welcome to Customer Support
full_text:
With the aim of ensuring a smooth and enjoyable travel experience, the Ministry of Civil Aviation has introduced DigiYatra, an initiative designed to facilitate paperless travel for passengers. This means passengers are no longer required to present physical tickets or boarding passes at different checkpoints, ultimately expediting the processing times and enhancing the overall journey.

To enrol in DigiYatra, passengers need to follow the steps outlined below:
1. Download the DigiYatra App from the Android or Apple store and use your Aadhaarlinked mobile number for registration.
2. Link your Aadhaar through Digi Locker. If you're not already registered with Digi Locker, please complete the registration using your Aadhaar number. It's crucial to ensure that your Aadhaar name matches your flight ticket details.
3. Capture a clear selfie, validate it with your Aadhaar, and securely save the provided credentials.
4. Add your boarding pass to the DigiYatra App before your flight and await confirmation.

Alternatively, you can register for DigiYatra at designated registration kiosks located at selected airports by following these steps:
1. Scan your boarding pass at the kiosk.
2. Complete the facial registration process.
3. Present a valid government-issued photo ID proof.
4. Proceed to the designated DigiYatra e-gates at various checkpoints.

This means passengers are no longer required to present physical tickets or boarding passes at different checkpoints, ultimately expediting the processing times and enhancing the overall journey. To enrol in DigiYatra, passengers need to follow the steps outlined below: 1. Download the DigiYatra App from the Android or Apple store and use your Aadhaarlinked mobile number for registration. 2. Link your Aadhaar through Digi Locker. If you're not already registered with Digi Locker, please complete the registration using your Aadhaar number. It's crucial to ensure that your Aadhaar name matches your flight ticket details. 3. Capture a clear selfie, validate it with your Aadhaar, and securely save the provided credentials. 4. Add your boarding pass to the DigiYatra App before your flight and await confirmation. Alternatively, you can register for DigiYatra at designated registration kiosks located at selected airports by following these steps: 1. Scan your boarding pass at the kiosk. 2. Complete the facial registration process. 3. Present a valid government-issued photo ID proof. 4. Proceed to the designated DigiYatra e-gates at various checkpoints.

title: Welcome to Customer Support
full_text:
Airline ticket fare rules govern the terms and conditions associated with the purchase, modification, and cancellation of airline tickets. 

Modify Booking:

1.  You can change your flight at any time from the time you make the booking till 2 hours prior to the scheduled time of departure

2. No Change fees will be retained by FLY91 if a Customer modifies a booking within 48 (forty eight) hours of its creation, provided the booking is for a flight departing no sooner than 7 (seven) days from the date of booking. However, any flight or date changes to a booking will necessitate Customers to pay any increase between the Tariff of the initial booking and the revised booking.
3. After 48 (forty eight) hours of creating a booking, Change fees per passenger per sector will be applicable as outlined in the Tariff Sheet available on the FLY91 website. 
4. In case of change of date/flight, the fare difference (if any) will be applicable in addition to the Change fee.

Cancel Booking 
1. You can perform any of the below cancellations in the booking from the time you make the booking till 2 hours prior to the scheduled time of departure: 

Cancel a single sector or flight 

Cancel multiple sectors or flights 

Cancel a single or more passengers within the booking 

Cancel the entire booking 

2. No Cancellation fees will be retained by FLY91 if a Customer cancels a booking within 48 (forty eight) hours of its creation, provided the booking is for a flight departing no sooner than 7 (seven) days from the date of booking.

3. After 48 (forty eight) hours of creating a booking, Cancellation Fees per passenger per sector will be applicable as outlined in the Tariff Sheet available on the FLY91 website. 
3. Convenience Fee is non-refundable 

4.  If a customer opts for a refund, it will be processed based on the payment method used for the booking and within specified timeframes.

No-Show

1. No changes can be made to bookings where the passenger is a no-show or fails to take the flight.

2. For no-show or missed flights, only the airport taxes will be refundable.

-->To modify or cancel your existing booking, please click <<here>>.

-->To raise a specific query or concern, please submit your request using the following link and we will contact you: Submit Ticket

For further information, please click here - Conditions of Carriage

title: Welcome to Customer Support
full_text:
FLY91 offers Group bookings facilitating the reservation of seats for large groups of passengers traveling together.

General Terms:

Group fares are applicable to a minimum of 7 Pax and above, No child fare applicable
Infant fare – INR 1500 per infant per sector
Group fares are not permitted to be redistributed on any other distribution platforms. Any instances of unauthorized dry selling will result in the full forfeiture of the paid amount.
The group quote is valid for 24 hours only.
Seats are subject to availability. The system will not hold seats till the PNR is created.
Re-routing (Change of sector) is not permitted on group booking.
Group PNR will be created within 24 hours post receipt of the payments.

Terms & Conditions – Agatti (AGX)

Deposit: 100% of the total fare quote.
Name List:  to be submitted D-30 days
Name Change: 3991 + Fare difference D-15 / 4991 + Fare Difference within 15 days of departure
Utilization: 100%
Refunds: Not Applicable

Terms & Condition – FLY91 Network except AGX

Deposit: 30% of the total fare quote on acceptance of the Group quote / If the Travel is within 15 days from the group quote acceptance, the 100% payment needs to be done in order to generate the PNR. Balance 70% needs to be done D-15
Name List: D-3 days
Name Change : Permitted upto 2 hours prior to the flight departure time and will incur a change fee of INR 2991 + any difference in fare prevalent at the time of modifying the booking.
Cancellation : 
Maximum of 20% of the seats in the booking (which shall not exceed 4 seats) and subject to the overall group size not reducing to below 7 seats is permitted upto 3 days prior to the flight departure at a cancellation fee of INR 2991 per passenger per sector. Any additional seat cancellation will be non-refundable.
In case of cancellation of the complete group booking upto 15 days prior to the flight departure date, the entire upfront advance payment will be retained.
In case of cancellation of the complete group booking within 15 days of the flight departure date, and/or for No-Shows, only applicable taxes will be refundable
Refunds, where applicable, are processed to the original source of payment used to make the booking
For Group Queries, please send us an email with your requirement at groups@fly91.in

title: Welcome to Customer Support
full_text:
FLY91 requires customers to present valid proof of identity for efficient travel and ticket validation. 

The following documents, carried in original, serve as proof of identity:

Passport.
Voter photo identity card issued by the Election Commission of India.
Aadhaar or m-Aadhaar issued by the Unique Identification Authority of India.
PAN card issued by the Income Tax Department.
Driving licence issued by the Regional Transport Offices.
Service photo identity card issued by State or Central Government, public sector undertakings, local bodies, or public limited companies.
Student photo identity cards issued by Government Institutions/Government recognized educational institutions.
Nationalised bank passbook with an attested photograph.
Pension card or any other pension-related documents having a photograph of the customer.
Photo identity cards or medical certificates issued to differently abled customers by the Governments of respective States or Union Territories.
For unaccompanied minors, any valid proof of identity from the above list is required.

If a customer is unable to produce any of the listed documents, an identity certificate issued by a Group A Gazetted Officer of the Central or State Government on official letterhead, with the customer's duly attested photograph, will be accepted as valid proof of identity.

-->To raise a specific query or concern, please submit your request using the following link and we will contact you: Submit Ticket​
